title: pilosella brachiata subsp. striatobrachiata
intro: Pilosella brachiata subsp. striatobrachiata, also known as Alpine Mouse Ear, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family. It is traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Pilosella brachiata subsp. striatobrachiata can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusions for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
